question_text: _____ what everyone assumed to be true.

options.A: The truly outstanding success of the principle of natural selection had been
options.B: Many biological ideas proposed during the past 150 years stood in stark conflict with
options.C: The theory of evolution by natural selection is thought to explain
options.D: Another aspect of the new philosophy of biology concerns
options.E: The scientific foundation created by Darwin has been

question_text: As space shuttles are designed to work in a vacuum, _____.

options.A: NASA engineers are hoping to cut take off weights by hall
options.B: about 85 percent of its weight is fuel
options.C: they have to carry not only fuel but also the oxygen to burn it
options.D: the method is obviously inefficient but better ones have yet to be found
options.E: such craft could accelerate to about ten times the speed of sound

question_text: _____ that is open to the amateur as well as the professional.

options.A: Today we know a lot about meteor showers and planetary weather
options.B: Astronomy is one of the few sciences
options.C: The mapping of solar, lunar and planetary surfaces has been accurately carried out
options.D: Thanks to advances in modern astronomy, more discoveries have been made about asteroids, comets, novae and supernovae
options.E: Through the use of computers and electronic imaging devices, our knowledge of the celestial constellations has grown rapidly

question_text: _____ how acid rain would affect trees.

options.A: The decreases in sulphur deposition has been so marked
options.B: The death of forests became a chief concern in the 1980s and led to dire predictions
options.C: There were many erroneous ideas about
options.D: In the 1980s and 1990s, there were concerns in the West
options.E: Some geologists have argued

question_text: Just as railway bridges were the great structural symbols of the 19th century, _____.

options.A: highway bridges became the engineering emblems of the 20th century
options.B: the invention of the automobile created an irresistible demand for better roads
options.C: most highway bridges carry lighter loads than railway bridges do
options.D: the type of bridge needed for cars and truck is fundamentally different from that needed for trains
options.E: an arch bridge curves upwards to support the roadway

question_text: Atomic clocks keep time by tracking the waves which atoms emit _____.

options.B: though there are other viable ways to improve it
options.C: because they lose only 1 second in 150 million years
options.D: before they can replace the clocks which are currently in use
options.E: while researchers have been working on an alternative
options.A: as they oscillate between different energy levels

question_text: _____, it might be able to perform far more complex tasks than today’s machines.

options.A: As the device is still in the early stages of development
options.B: Although no such computer as yet exists
options.C: Once the theory has been adequately tested
options.D: Unless there is fresh evidence to the country
options.E: If such a computer could be made

question_text: There are seasons of plentiful water and others of scarcity _____.

options.A: when water has to be used sparingly
options.B: though irrigation schemes were introduced
options.C: since these outlets are designed to deliver supplies of water automatically
options.D: where traditional methods of irrigation still remain in use
options.E: so flow irrigation is best suited to a flat countryside

question_text: More attention is being given to improved efficiency in the use of fuel _____.

options.A: because they cause environmental pollution
options.B: if prices continue to rise at this rate
options.C: as the cost of fuel increases
options.D: whether cleaner methods are forthcoming
options.E: which should have been discovered earlier

question_text: After take-off, the pilot of an aircraft is often given a course to steer _____.

options.A: until a specific reporting point or height is reached
options.B: though the air traffic control officer will still be using field glasses
options.C: whether the weather conditions were suitable or not
options.D: since he will be watched by direct visual means from the control tower unless there is fog
options.E: so long as the landing gear retracts correctly

question_text: Why didn’t you remind him ______?

options.A: not to have overlooked the possibility of a breakdown in the electric circuit
options.B: having stabilized the pressure to a reasonable extent
options.C: to check the temperature of the solution at regular intervals
options.D: the printer will be checked for compatibility with the microcomputer
options.E: to have got rid of all the outdated equipment

question_text: _____ as it contains salt.

options.A: The sea freezes at temperatures below the normal freezing point of water
options.B: She tries to avoid such types of food
options.C: In polar regions glaciers frequently flow down into the sea
options.D: The warmer the air the more moisture it can carry
options.E: Snowflakes, like other forms of water vapour, nearly always possess a crystal structure

question_text: The collapse of a British company developing genetically engineered vaccines in plants has raised fears _____.

options.A: why the public has shown so much hostility to genetically modified plants
options.B: that medical biotechnology may also be adversely affected
options.C: if industry analysists had been intimidated by rival companies
options.D: unless all such plants are grown in glass houses and not in open fields
options.E: even though it failed to raise adequate funds to finance the next stage of its vaccine development programme

question_text: _____, because there was a shortage of coal for smelting.

options.A: In Japan, steel expansion has been subsidized for several years
options.B: Italy has only very limited supplies of iron ore
options.C: In Sweden, steel-making on a large scale developed late
options.D: French reserves are among the best in Europe as regards quantity
options.E: Stimulated by the rapid growth of the railways, the iron and steel industry expanded rapidly in Russia.

question_text: Insect-eating plants have devices _____.

options.A: from which stickiness an insect can never make its escape
options.B: if their prey is to be enticed into the trap
options.C: of which the pitcher plant that produces pepsin is an example
options.D: which enable them to catch insects and digest them with the aid of enzymes
options.E: so that they are able to live in most parts of the world but chiefly in warm regions

question_text: For a small planet, the Earth is extremely heavy _____.

options.B: that two-thirds of it are covered with ocean
options.C: that the atmosphere screens the sun’s radiation
options.D: if the atmosphere were not rich in oxygen
options.E: since its iron core is very large
options.A: whether the liquid iron creates a powerful magnetic field

question_text: The old system of flood irrigation has fallen into disrepute _____.

options.A: unless short canals could have enabled the compartments to be filled in an orderly fashion
options.B: though the flood waters had been controlled to cover so much land
options.C: as the height of the flood was variable so was the area flooded
options.D: after several weeks the water would be drained back into the river
options.E: because it only produces one crop a year

question_text: _____ when heat resistant materials are required.

options.A: Engineers often use ceramics
options.B: There are two main types of metal; ferrous and non-ferrous
options.C: With the addition of chromium, the steel’s ability to resist corrosion was increased
options.D: Both bronze and brass have been used ornamentally
options.E: Cutting tools are made from high-speed steels

question_text: Helium, the lightest of the inert gases, was discovered in 1868 _____.

options.A: until its importance was recognized in scientific research at low temperatures
options.B: as liquid helium has many remarkable qualities which are only imperfectly understood
options.C: when spectrographic investigations disclosed an unknown yellow line in the chromosphere of the sun
options.D: before studies into atomic structure would be realized
options.E: though two of its stable isotopes exist as liquids right down to the absolute zero

question_text: _____, the Earth’s crust is actually in a state of continual flux.

options.B: Whenever there are sudden changes in temperature
options.C: However unreliable the method has proved
options.D: When such an event is least expected
options.E: Though it may not appear to be so
options.A: If only they had been better prepared

question_text: X-rays are generated _____.

options.E: because the radiation was being emitted at a tangent
options.A: if the sun is a copious source of energy
options.B: when electrical charges are accelerated or decelerated
options.C: after the target had been bombarded by electrons
options.D: until the circuit gave off practically pure direct current

question_text: A chief defect of wire ropes is fatigue _____.

options.A: that stress is set up by these vibrations
options.B: as if there has been a gradual development of transverse cracks
options.C: although the elastic limit of wire ropes declined with use
options.D: which is induced by the vibrations set up in hosting or lowering loads
options.E: if the maximum load had been exceeded

question_text: _____ even though it was obviously very limited in scope.

options.A: The experiment he has recently been engaged in has produced some interesting results
options.B: His paper aroused considerable interest
options.C: The research project will be assigned to a team of specialists
options.D: The hypothesis will finally be put to the test
options.E: His intention will, in all likelihood, be misunderstood

question_text: While the Captain was working on that film about underwater life, _____.

options.A: a whole new fascinating world has opened up for him
options.B: his team of divers are taken ill
options.C: he accidentally discovered the wreck of an old ship
options.D: he had been attracted by sharks
options.E: his boat will be in danger of drifting ashore

question_text: _____ which expands when heated.

options.A: A thermometer contains mercury
options.B: This is a characteristic of all metals
options.C: The atmosphere contains various gases
options.D: Parts of the sea floor remain unexpected
options.E: The hole in the ozone layer is becoming more and more dangerous
